title: American Chocolate Chip Cookies

subs.name: whole wheat flour (atta) for a nuttier biscuit; add 1 extra tbsp of butter
same weight in coconut oil for a dairy-free version; skip the browning step

ingredients.name: sugar
visual_checks: use brown sugar if available for chewier cookies; white works fine
ingredients.notes: use brown sugar if available for chewier cookies; white works fine

subs.name: 3 tbsp thick curd (dahi) for an eggless version; cookies will be softer and may spread less

ingredients.name: dark chocolate chips or chopped dark chocolate

subs.name: Morde dark compound chocolate (widely available in India), chopped — works perfectly

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 175°C. Line a baking tray with parchment paper or grease it lightly with butter.

Melt the butter in a saucepan on medium flame. Keep cooking, swirling occasionally, until it turns a golden-amber colour and smells nutty — about 4 minutes. Pour into a mixing bowl and allow to cool for 5 minutes.
Whisk the sugar into the browned butter until combined. Beat in the egg and vanilla and whisk for about 1 minute until the mixture thickens slightly and turns pale.
Stir in the maida, baking soda and salt until a dough forms — do not overmix. Fold in the chocolate chips or chopped chocolate.
Scoop tablespoon-sized balls of dough onto the prepared baking tray, spacing them at least 5 cm apart as they spread. Gently press each ball to flatten slightly.
Bake at 175°C for 10–12 minutes until the edges are golden and set but the centre looks underdone and shiny — this is correct. The cookies will firm up as they cool.
Cool on the tray for 5 minutes before lifting — they are fragile when hot. Transfer to a wire rack. Eat warm for the gooey chocolate experience or store in an airtight tin.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: butter → same weight in coconut oil for a dairy-free version; skip the browning step; cocoa → Morde dark compound chocolate (widely available in India), chopped — works perfectly; egg → 3 tbsp thick curd (dahi) for an eggless version; cookies will be softer and may spread less; maida → whole wheat flour (atta) for a nuttier biscuit; add 1 extra tbsp of butter
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Any standard OTG oven works well at 175°C. For households without an oven, use a thick kadai: place a layer of salt in the base, put a stand inside, rest the baking tray on the stand, cover and bake on medium-low flame for 15–18 minutes. Morde dark chocolate is the go-to chip substitute available at any baking supply store in India.
